title: BizLink to buy Blackstone's Interplex ICT unit for $850M
subtitle: Taiwan's BizLink Holding will acquire Blackstone's Interplex Datacom, an information and communications technology business, for $850 million. The deal expands BizLink's footprint in ICT components and connectors.

title: Cambridge Aerospace in Talks to Raise at $3.5 Billion Valuation
subtitle: Cambridge Aerospace is raising approximately $300 million at a $3.5 billion valuation. The British startup builds interceptor systems to counter drones and cruise missiles, reflecting strong investor appetite for European defense technology.

title: Anthropic ships Claude Fable 5 in two safety tiers
subtitle: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5 (generally available with classifiers) and Claude Mythos 5 (limited), both built on the same model with different safeguards. Mythos 5 lifts cyber restrictions via Project Glasswing, a new restricted-access tier for risk-tolerant users.

title: Anthropic pulls Bedrock traffic outside AWS security boundary
subtitle: Anthropic will require AWS Bedrock customers to retain and share 30 days of traffic data on Mythos and Fable 5 models. The policy extends to all future models of similar or higher capability to detect misuse patterns.

title: TSMC sales jump 30% as AI chip demand holds
subtitle: TSMC reported monthly sales rose 30%, driven by sustained global demand for AI chips. The figure underscores how the AI infrastructure build-out continues to fuel Taiwan's top chipmaker's revenue growth.

title: India freezes Starlink approvals over Iran war terminal concerns
subtitle: India has frozen approvals for Starlink, citing concerns that satellite terminals are being used in the Iran war. The freeze creates a new obstacle for Elon Musk's internet service in one of its largest potential markets.

title: Credit Agricole CEO breaks with peers on AI job cuts
subtitle: Credit Agricole CEO Olivier Gavalda is pushing back against AI anxiety in banking, calling for fears to end as many executives warn of workforce reductions. His stance puts him at odds with peers who predict AI will shrink headcount in the sector.

title: Mega IPOs crowd out mid-market SaaS, Bravo says
subtitle: Orlando Bravo, Thoma Bravo's co-founder, said the 'SaaSpocalypse' SaaS downturn is finished at Berlin's SuperReturn conference. He noted slower PE deal activity and warned smaller firms struggle for attention as mega IPOs dominate.

title: Heavy JavaScript killed this utility’s form
subtitle: A developer rebuilt their site using a server-rendered HTML approach and saw their user count double in a single night. The result suggests that dropping heavy JavaScript frameworks can pay off sharply for content-focused sites.

title: Late-stage AI returns widen venture market's two-tier split
subtitle: Later-stage AI startup investments are now generating higher returns than in previous years, according to Bloomberg analysis. The improved math is drawing more capital to mature AI firms despite broader startup valuation concerns.

title: Cosco locks in 50-year grip on Tarragona port
subtitle: Cosco has been awarded a 50-year port concession in Tarragona on Spain's east coast as car imports through the country climb. The long-term deal gives the Chinese shipping giant an entrenched foothold in a key Mediterranean hub.

title: Economists reach for 1860s coal theory to explain AI job fears
subtitle: Economists are applying the 1860s Jevons Paradox to AI, arguing efficiency gains may boost rather than reduce labor demand. The theory, originally about coal consumption, complicates predictions of widespread job losses from automation.
